_meta.title: Creality (stock OS) — native print state → normalized lifecycle
_meta.confidence: 🟡 source-read (grouping); ⚪ inferred for the fine 6/7/8 labels

derived_from.source_note: Clean-room from Creality's own committed device-manager frontend inside their OrcaSlicer fork. No code copied; no secrets. No stock device bench-validated in this orchard.

notes: `state` is an int on the flat pushed field-bag. Do NOT conflate it with `deviceState`, a separate online/health int (`-1` offline / `0` online-idle / `1` busy).
Fine labels for `6`/`7`/`8` are low-confidence (⚪) — a live idle→print→pause→complete→fail capture on a stock K1/KE would close them.

Write-safety should derive from the COMPOSITE read (`printFileName` + `printProgress` + `err.errcode` + `selfTestStep`) plus the busy set `{1,5,6,7,8}`, never from `state` alone. The frontend treats `{1,5,8}` as print-in-progress and `{5,6}` as paused.

mappings.note: paused-side per the write-gate `{5,6}`; fine label unconfirmed
printing-side per the write-gate `{1,5,8}` (recovery?); fine label unconfirmed
